hero.heading: I build systems that last.
hero.subheading: Started in neuroscience, pivoted through a professional kitchen, landed in software. Now I architect distributed systems and obsess over developer experience.

neuroscience.title: Bachelor in Neuroscience
neuroscience.summary: Started studying the brain — fell in love with complex systems and emergent behaviour. Switched tracks when I realised building systems was closer to what I actually wanted to do.

cooking.title: Professional kitchen
cooking.summary: Two years behind the stove. Learned precision, composure under pressure, and that craft takes time. Carried that into every line of code since.

ecole42.title: École 42
ecole42.summary: Peer-to-peer engineering school with no teachers, no lectures. Built a C compiler, a shell, a container runtime, and a web server from scratch. Graduated top of cohort.

resilioJunior.title: Software Engineer
resilioJunior.summary: Joined a 12-person startup building LCA tooling. Shipped the first version of the calculation engine, the data pipeline, and the API layer.

resilioSenior.title: Senior Engineer
resilioSenior.summary: Led the architecture migration from monolith to service-oriented. Hired and mentored two engineers. Drove observability, testing discipline, and platform reliability.

projects.title: Side projects & open source
projects.summary: Built MCPMEM (MCP memory extension), Prompt Pilot (prompt management CLI), Voltaire (French grammar checker), and a handful of experiments in LLM orchestration.

present.title: Now
present.summary: Designing the next generation of the platform. Thinking about data models, API contracts, and how to build systems that outlast their original authors.

contact.heading: Let's make something worth the commit.
contact.subheading: Open to interesting problems, collaborations, and the occasional coffee chat. Reach out however feels right.
